items.title: Processed Food Won‚Äôt Kill You
items.author: Kurzgesagt ‚Äď In a Nutshell
items.content:

items.title: How Are Memories Stored Inside Your Brain?
items.author: Kurzgesagt ‚Äď In a Nutshell
items.content:
<article>Avoid your blindspots and get every side of every story at https://ground.news/nutshell. Subscribe for 40% off unlimited access.

Brighten your space with our new human-made & high-quality Neon Framed Art Prints: https://shop.kgs.link/faves ūüíõ Shop these and more of our handpicked favorites in the kurzgesagt shop!

Sources & further reading:
https://sites.google.com/view/sources-memory/

Memory is one of the strangest abilities you possess. Your brain uses an incredibly complex biological system to preserve moments from your past that no longer exist, allowing you to revisit experiences from years ago. But memories are not always recordings of reality. In fact, every time a memory comes under the spotlight of your attention, it can melt and change a tiny bit.

What exactly is a memory, how is a moment in time stored inside your brain, and why does remembering something slowly rewrite the story of your life?

items.title: Let‚Äôs Steal a New Moon
items.author: Kurzgesagt ‚Äď In a Nutshell
items.content:
<article>What if Earth could swap its Moon for a better one? From volcanic Io to methane-soaked Titan, the Solar System offers some tempting alternatives. But maybe our Moon is already perfect.

items.title: Your Brain Peaked at Birth
items.author: Kurzgesagt ‚Äď In a Nutshell
items.content:
<article>Babies may seem helpless, but their brains are incredible learning machines. They can hear sounds from any language, recognize faces adults can't, and build millions of neural connections every second. ūüß†ūüĎ∂
